regimeNotes: RISK-ON confirmed and extending. Equities: June 15 close S&P 500 +1.65 pct to 7554.29, Nasdaq +3.07 pct to 26683.94 (best day since Mar 31), Dow +0.92 pct to a record 51671.03 -- broad rally continuing on the Iran peace deal / oil collapse. NVDA +3.54 pct to 212.45; SPY to 754.83 (position now +1.78 pct above the 741.75 cost basis). DXY soft near 99.56 (USD weakening); EURUSD 1.1594. 10Y yield ~4.47-4.48 pct, down a touch and still easing. GLD +2.59 pct to 396.55 despite risk-on -- an unusual broad rally (gold + equities up, oil down together) reflecting geopolitical-premium unwind plus dovish Fed repricing rather than a regime conflict. Crypto: BTC 66,529 USD (+1.3 pct vs yesterday's 65,662), ETH 1,789.91 (+4.0 pct), SOL 74.86 (+4.9 pct) -- alts outperforming BTC, dominance ticked down to 56.49 pct from 56.60 pct. Total crypto mcap 2.36T (+1.17 pct 24h). Crypto Fear and Greed still Extreme Fear at 23 -- sentiment lagging price strength by a wide margin (classic contrarian-bullish divergence), but the sleeve is already at 21.0 pct NAV, comfortably mid-way to the 35 pct risk-on cap, so no fresh capital was added today. KEY CATALYST AHEAD: Kevin Warsh's first FOMC meeting as Fed Chair concludes tomorrow June 17 -- CME FedWatch implies a 96 pct probability of a hold at 3.50-3.75 pct, but it is also a Summary of Economic Projections meeting (new dot plot) plus his first press conference, so messaging risk is real and two-sided. DECISION: HOLD, zero trades today. Per yesterday's logged post-FOMC plan, new-money decisions (NVDA entry, ETH add) are deliberately held until after Warsh's press conference rather than front-running a brand-new Fed chair's first major communication. All existing theses intact, no invalidation levels close to being breached. BTC is already near the 15 pct single-entry cap (14.66 pct NAV) so no further BTC adds regardless of FOMC outcome. Cash buffer 19,500.12 USD = 19.15 pct NAV, comfortably above the 5 pct floor and available to deploy once the Fed signal is in hand.

SPY.thesis: Core US equity beta in a confirmed risk-on regime. SPY at 754.83 (+1.78 pct above 741.75 cost), tracking S&P 500's best multi-day run since the Iran deal. Oil-collapse consumer tailwind plus improved Fed flexibility still the drivers.
SPY.invalidationLevel: Sustained close below 660 USD or confirmed Fed re-pricing toward additional hikes.

MSFT.thesis: AI/cloud quality compounder at 399.76 (+2.31 pct on the day). Strong FCF; Nasdaq-led rally suits MSFT. Hold and let it compound through FOMC.
MSFT.invalidationLevel: Cloud growth deceleration or AI capex without demonstrable ROI; close below 340 USD.

GOOGL.thesis: Quality cash machine with AI/search optionality at 369.35 (+2.69 pct on the day). Regime favors growth names; still a reasonable multiple vs. peers.
GOOGL.invalidationLevel: Search share erosion to AI rivals; close below 310 USD.

UNH.thesis: Contrarian defensive value at 411.04 (+1.19 pct), stabilizing after the risk-on rotation pressure. Analyst upgrades intact (BofA 450, MS 453, JPM 466); durable earnings and dividend growth unchanged.
UNH.invalidationLevel: Further regulatory or medical-cost shock; close below 360 USD.

TLT.thesis: Long-duration Treasuries at 85.72, roughly flat. Thesis depends on Warsh signaling patience tomorrow; a hawkish dot-plot surprise (hold-but-no-cuts-this-year tone) is the key near-term risk to monitor post-FOMC.
TLT.invalidationLevel: 10Y yield decisively above 5 pct (Fed forced to hike again).

GLD.thesis: Debasement and tail-risk hedge at 396.55 (+2.59 pct), an outstanding performer since entry. Soft USD and unwind of geopolitical premium both still constructive even as equities rally in parallel.
GLD.invalidationLevel: Real yields spike with strong USD reversal; close below 350 USD.

BTC.thesis: Core crypto sleeve leader at 66,529 USD (avgCost 64,629; 0.224359 BTC). Position now 14.66 pct NAV, near the 15 pct single-entry cap, so no further adds regardless of FOMC outcome. F&G still Extreme Fear (23) despite the price rally -- sentiment lagging is itself a constructive signal.

ETH.thesis: Discounted L1 leader at 1,789.91 (+4.0 pct on the day), outperforming BTC as dominance slips. ETH dominance still low historically; thesis strengthening but a planned add is held until after tomorrow's FOMC per the logged plan.
ETH.invalidationLevel: Structural loss of share to L1 rivals; close below 1,300 USD.

SOL.thesis: Small high-beta satellite at 74.86 (+4.9 pct on the day), the best-performing holding today. Sized deliberately small (2.17 pct NAV) for narrative upside optionality; no change.
SOL.invalidationLevel: Break below 52 USD; network deterioration or sentiment collapse.
